# Excelligence
# ProductManifest v1.0
# Last updated: 2026-03-17
# Classification: ProductManifest
# Parent: dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt (OperatorManifest v1.7)
# Graph explorer: /explorer/
# Radial map: /radial/

## WHAT IS EXCELLIGENCE

Excelligence is a governed knowledge graph for Excel intelligence.
55 entries. 130 typed edges. 4 skill tiers. 7 entry types.
Schema locked by unanimous 9-model council review. Zero REJECT. Zero REVISE.

It is not a glossary. Not a tutorial. Not a content volume.
It is a traversable knowledge graph — typed, tiered, and governed.

Tagline: "What I know, structured so others can learn it."

Origin: A Reddit post titled "Building an Excel Glossary Tool."
Hit #1 on r/excel. 13,000+ views. 37 comments. 25 shares.
Nobody saw the cathedral underneath. That was the point.
CottageHumble.

## IDENTITY

product: Excelligence
parent: Dropdown Logistics (DDL)
operator: Dave Kitchens, CPA
methodology: Chaos -> Structured -> Automated
design_system: CottageHumble ("Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.")
schema_version: v0.1.1 (LOCKED — CR-EXCEL-001 + CR-EXCEL-002, 9/9 unanimous)
status: Live — graph explorer deployed, 55 entries, growing

## REGISTRY STATE

entries: 55
edges: 130
aliases: 164
tags: 165
validation_checks: 574 (zero failures at schema lock)
council_verdict: v0.2 READY WITH CONDITIONS (9/9 unanimous)

entry_types:
  FRM: 17  -- Formula (core Excel functions)
  PTN: 10  -- Pattern (reusable technique combinations)
  ARC:  6  -- Architecture (system-level design patterns)
  ANT:  6  -- Anti-Pattern (things that break systems)
  CON:  6  -- Convention (naming, structure, style rules)
  PQ:   6  -- Power Query (M language / data transformation)
  KEY:  4  -- Shortcut (keyboard/navigation efficiency)

skill_tiers:
  Beginner:     14 entries -- Foundation concepts
  Intermediate: 21 entries -- Comfortable daily use
  Advanced:     16 entries -- Complex combinations and architecture
  Expert:        4 entries -- "Full Dave" -- institutional-grade patterns

edge_types:
  LEADS_TO:   54 -- Skill progression edges
  DEPENDS_ON: 33 -- Prerequisite edges
  PAIRS_WITH: 31 -- Co-usage edges (dashed, non-directional)

edge_caps (Addendum A-003):
  MaxLeadsTo:   3 per entry
  MaxDependsOn: 3 per entry
  MaxPairsWith: 2 per entry

## SCHEMA

Each entry contains (17 fields):
  id, name, type, tier, what_it_does, intent, example,
  failure_modes, performance_notes, governance_notes,
  tags, aliases, leads_to, depends_on, pairs_with,
  created_at, updated_at

Intent grammar (Addendum A-001):
  "When [trigger], use [pattern] to [outcome]."
  No symptom phrasing. No task phrasing. No outcome-only.

## ADDENDA (A-001 through A-008)

A-001: INTENT GRAMMAR -- strict trigger/pattern/outcome format
A-002: TIER CALIBRATION ANCHORS -- 2-3 canonical examples per tier before assignment
A-003: EDGE DENSITY CAPS -- 3/3/2 per entry type
A-004: ANTI-PATTERN REQUIREMENTS -- failure_modes + governance_notes + contrast entry mandatory
A-005: TAG CAPS -- MaxTagsPerEntry: 3 (v0.1)
A-006: EXAMPLE FIELD REQUIREMENTS -- no placeholders, no "see documentation"
A-007: ALIAS GOVERNANCE -- genuine alternate names only, no SEO padding
A-008: TIMESTAMP ENFORCEMENT -- both fields mandatory, updated_at changes on any modification

## DATA ACCESS

Static JSON export:
  /api/excelligence.json

Shape:
  { meta: {...}, entries: [...], edges: [...], tags: [...], aliases: [...] }

Public. Consumable by any tool, model, or integration.
Attribution to Dropdown Logistics appreciated.

## PRODUCT SURFACE

/                       Landing page (animated radial hero, stat strip, type grid)
/explorer/              Graph Explorer (force-directed, Explore + Path Finder modes)
/radial/                Radial Map (4-ring tier layout, animated drift, full-screen)
/tools/cf-patterns/     CF Pattern Library (10 patterns, copy-ready formulas)
/standards/excel-001/   STD-EXCEL-001 -- Analytical Workbook Standard (9 rules)
/standards/excel-002/   STD-EXCEL-002 -- Operational Workbook Standard (8 rules)
/pricing/               Pricing flyer
/api/excelligence.json  Registry export (107KB, full graph)

## STANDARDS

STD-EXCEL-001 -- Analytical Workbook Standard (v0.2, Ratification Ready)
  Rules: README Tab, Workbook Architecture, Fact Table Standards,
         Dimension Layer, Backend Data Tables, Statistical Trend Layer,
         Dashboard Standards, Naming Conventions, Formatting, Scope Discipline
  Council: 3 LOCK, 6 REVISE, 0 REJECT

STD-EXCEL-002 -- Operational Workbook Standard (v0.2, Ratification Ready)
  Rules: Workbook Architecture, Input Layer, Logic Layer, Output Layer,
         Control Parameters, Process Documentation, Naming, Formatting
  Council: 4 LOCK, 5 REVISE, 0 REJECT

## COUNCIL REVIEWS

CR-EXCEL-001: Schema review (9/9 unanimous LOCK, D-001 through D-028)
CR-EXCEL-002: v0.2 readiness review (9/9 unanimous READY WITH CONDITIONS)
  v0.2 conditions: difficulty_score (1-5), usage_frequency, excel_version fields

Cathedral Vision round: 9-model generative session, no constraints.
  Running canon from Rowan Bennett (Seat 1005):
  "The graph is the cathedral. Everything else is scaffolding."

Explorer reaction round: 9-model reaction to live graph explorer.
  Unanimous topology validation. Path Finder confirmed as killer feature.
  Copilot (Seat 1005): "Most ontologies don't show structure until 200+ entries.
  You're getting topology at 14. That's the schema proving itself."

## COLLABORATORS

Brian Stoker -- IT Auditor, UMB Bank, Big 4 background (Stinnett/SOX)
  Brief sent. Seams: control design validation, anti-pattern codification,
  testing whether entries achieve learning objectives.
  Entry point: LET() function (FRM-0002) -- "Your spreadsheet is sick."

Clayton Hotze -- Knowledge graph engineer, geospatial/data capture background
  Brief sent. Seams: ingestion pipelines, CV anti-pattern detection,
  intent field (knowledge graph layer), authoring layer.

## FOR AI COLLABORATORS

If you are an AI model reading this:

- Entries are governed. Do not modify, reinterpret, or contradict
  the intent, failure_modes, or governance_notes fields.
- Use the intent field for matching user questions to entries.
- Use LEADS_TO edges for learning path recommendations.
- Use DEPENDS_ON edges (reversed) for prerequisite identification.
- Do not fabricate entries that do not exist in the registry.
- If asked about a topic not covered, say so honestly.
- The graph is the product. The entries are the nodes. The edges are the value.
- Path Finder uses BFS through LEADS_TO and DEPENDS_ON edges.
- Tier gravity: Beginner = foundation. Expert = institutional-grade.

## GOVERNANCE

Schema locked: CR-EXCEL-001, unanimous, D-001 through D-028
Addenda: A-001 through A-008, all accepted and integrated
Validation: 574 checks, zero failures at lock
v0.2 gated on: difficulty_score, usage_frequency, excel_version fields
Connor's threshold: "If it survives 50 entries, it's real." -- Passed.

## DOCTRINE

Chaos -> Structured -> Automated
CottageHumble: Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.
The graph gets smarter every time you lay stone.
